item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While you're completely spot on when it comes to whether or not the skip objectively needs the 911 call log, I very much subjectively think it needs it because I got to write "What're they gonna do, arrest the duck?", and that still makes me chuckle. ;)

Glad you like the concept though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the concept of this, but it's one of the most stark examples of SCP wiki pointless interview log syndrome.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so if I was Researcher Johnny Foundation, and I was reading this article, my first thought about the vanishing of the dogs as they get swallowed by the ducks would be "Hey, maybe there is a portal in there during these events." SCP-1763 makes reference to a extradimensional portal classification system, so making a link there could work in such a reference. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Once swallowed, prey does not travel down the esophagus, instead disappearing from SCP-2250-1 altogether. Attempts to track test subjects have almost exclusively yielded a location approximately 6.2 km beneath the island of Moloka'i. Attempts to reach this location have as of yet proven fruitless.

To this:

Once swallowed, prey does not travel down the esophagus, instead disappearing from SCP-2250-1 altogether. Attempts to track test subjects have almost exclusively yielded a location approximately 6.2 km beneath the island of Moloka'i1. Attempts to reach this location have as of yet proven fruitless.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Whether a type of inter-dimensional portal, as observed in SCP-1763, or similar phenomena is responsible for apparent teleportation is currently under investigation.

item.title: A reference?
item.content:encoded: This SCP's code name is "Duck Hunt". It involves ducks and dogs. Now where's that Zapper? (Coincidentally, another SCP is a Zapper.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to leave this skip alone, then it happened. The Foundation is actively going out there and giving people a copy of their eaten doggies and calling it a day. I lost my shit. Congratulations.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit. Now I feel obligated to make the Tale, and I hate that show.
I'm gonna start watching it, and when my family starts asking about the change in behavior, I'm just gonna be all, "I'm doing research."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I smell a Project Crossover tale. Go for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm calling it. The cast of Duck Dynasty are actually Foundation operatives, running a front company and containing this shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not, actually. It just happened to be a great pun. Thanks for your upvote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this whole thing supposed to be a gigantic reference to the game duck hunt? Also +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The implications for humans are a bit worse. Would you like to get a job as an impromptu eldritch feeding hole?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In fact, note that the containment procedures dictate that any attempt to decipher the ducks' communication must be stopped. That implies that the waterfowl have somehow stumbled across some concept which, if understood by humans, would have dire implications for dogkind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how this implies that ducks may already have the ability to create dog eating tentacles within them, and this memetic scp simply allows them to harness this power!
or am i reading that wrong?
either way, this really messed me up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The notion that this is correctly labelled memetic is a lot wierder and creepier that saying that it just has a memetic vector. That suggests that whatever idea is being shared is like a key in a preexisting locked door in the minds of ducks. EVERY duck is an anomalous Lovecraftian gateway… we're just lucky most of them haven't realized it yet!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll never be able to play Duck Hunt again without thinking of this…

…fuct! (But a +1 in spite of poor dogs dying.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has been feexed, finally. Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Officer, arrest that duck! +1
P.S.

Despite this invasive and potential for extensive physical trauma, […]

Still missing something there. I'd fix it, but I can't figure out what you were trying to say exactly (making any possible edit too substantial a fix for a non-author).

But still, it's great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the duck? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem is the sheer timescale involved - the average dog life span is around 10 years, give or take a few, but mostly give. It would likely leave the people with very inconsistent memories and a good deal of cognitive dissonance - I mean, think of petting your dog and like, not being able to escape the pervasive thought he used to be bigger and somewhat paler in colour.

That said, this just about works as a misguided effort on part of the site director and actually makes it creepier. Not the least, my parents apparently did this to me once with a hampster when I was about 5 or so - quietly replaced the one that had died after he's escaped. I noticed a number of differences, mainly that his slightly lame leg had healed, but with a bit of nudging from them I ascribed it to the grasshoppers I've been feeding him that summer having such a positive effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Despite this invasive and potential for extensive physical trauma,"

Think you're missing a word there. Otherwise A+, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, 10/10 was not aware, would do research in the future. I'll edit that, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
87% of its DNA

Emmm, are you aware how much is the difference between our DNA and other animal's DNA? that 13% is pretty much the difference between flying squirrels and fireflies1.

E: I rechecked and 2-3% percent is what makes humans and chmps different, and 10% is what separate us from cats.

E2: I don't enjoy that something that affects ducks to pretty much produce tentacles out of their mouse and eat dogs is beng considered merely memetic in the article.

Footnotes
1. probably more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Easier replacing the dog, than finding every single person that may know about the dog and can ask about it.
'Hey, where's Bobby?' 'Who's Bobby?' '…your dog?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this. 10 out of 10! Would snuggle and feed dogs to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your last sentence is my point. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From Understanding Memetics:

"A memetic SCP would be more likely to be a phrase that makes you think you have wings as opposed to a phrase that makes you actually grow a pair of wings. If you write up magic words that make people grow wings, it should be described as something other than memetic."

It sounds to me that a magic duck call that makes ducks grow dimensional portals in their mouths probably isn't memetic in the same way words that cause a person to grow wings probably aren't memetic. On the other hand it does only say more likely at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It will cease movement, open its beak, and produce a series of sounds in the 25 - 32 kHz range.

I can't get the image of duck speakers out of my head now. It just opens its beak and friggin' dubstep or something starts blasting.

Strange images aside, I love this. It's that perfect balance of strange but unsettling. And it's nice to see something that doesn't affect humans for once. I personally liked the audio log, serious SCP's that can make me laugh always win brownie points in my books.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What in the absolute shitting hell.

So this starts with an inversion of the standard duck call->dog flushes ducks out->hunter shoots cycle, and ends with dogs being dragged into the Hawaiian underworld. I can dig it. I'm particularly impressed that this is a non-human meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure why you believe this to be anything but memetic. Ducks communicate amongst themselves, thereby triggering the phenomenon. It's a misconception that memetic contagions definitely cannot have physical effects, though they usually don't. As for the duck thing: nowhere does it say only human beings are susceptible to memetic threats.

Unless I'm missing something, in which case I'd like to hear what that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was entertaining, but my primary criticism is that this anomaly is basically described as memetic (which is already a bit of a stretch because, y'know, ducks), but has a non-memetic effect (summons Cthulhu Jr. via duck-hole). Something about that doesn't sit right with me.

May I suggest either clarifying that the vector is memetic but the effect is not, or simply pulling the memetic description altogether? Trying to reconcile this pulled me out of the story several times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point taken. It's been edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amnestics, at least as generally used, make you forget a period of time. It can't pinpoint memories and erase, say, every memory of your dog. It can make you forget that your dog was swallowed whole by a duck, but not that you ever had one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only issue is that it is implausible (very unlikely, yet still possible) for GPS to give you coordinates 6.2km below the surface because there is so much obstruction. It is absolutely impossible for it to give you coordinates anywhere except on Earth because that's not the way GPS works.

I'd just change out your use of "GPS" to "tracking system".

Fix that and I'll give ya +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation as a whole would probably not condone replacing victims' pets, no. I put that in because I could see the project lead for 2250 being a dog owner themselves, and wanting to in even a little insignificant way, take a bit of the coldness out of The Foundation's demeanor. Take that as you will. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's say they are from the same universe and call it a day. E: I was referring to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like the idea. Like Thekillerax said, it's bizarre without being too much random. Little creatures swallowing big ones always made me shudder :) And I don't think the audio log is useless. In my opinion it perfectly adds to the creepyness of the object.

One last thing:

Should these civilians have lost their dog to SCP-2250, they are to be supplied with a suitable replacement and led to believe it is the original.

I'm not sure the Foundation would buy them new dogs. I think they'll just give them amnestics and make them forget they had pets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Try to think of them as convenient portals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that I re-read it…birds with tentacles? That's suspicious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was too perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked how one dog corpse was found floating in the:

Pelican Nebula

Bird pun. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, more avian SCPs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't suppose the interview adds much, no. I guess I just really liked writing the sentence "what're they gonna do? Arrest the duck?". Sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think the interview log added anything interesting. Other than that, this is pretty good. It skirted the line of being too "lolrandom" but managed to find that equilibrium of bizarre enough to be unsettling but not laughable. I especially liked the part where the GPS signal was originating from space. To me it felt like the Eldritch dog-eating memetic abomination knows it's been caught and is attempting to ward off its discoverers by giving them faulty coordinates, but is so unfamiliar with how our world works that it doesn't know that the cool misdirection spot isn't actually going to work. The mental picture was pretty fucked up too. And I thought ducks were creepy enough…
+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is an attempt to do three things:

1. Get that image of a dog being swallowed whole by a duck out of my head.
2. Write a memetic threat that doesn't target humans.
3. Write an article about a weird, semi-eldritch thing that for once doesn't eat humans. Yet.

Thanks to TwistedGears and djkaktus for looking this over in chat. If you're [PLACEHOLDER], whack me upside the head in Wikidot PM so I can give you proper credit. I M Dumb.

Edit: Yeah, I M Dumb alright.
